mitre: T1059.007 — Command and Scripting Interpreter: JavaScript
description: Injection of malicious client-side scripts into vulnerable web application inputs.

cvss_methodology.note: Each scenario is scored independently using the CVSS v3.1 Base Score formula. The vector reflects the worst-case conditions of that specific scenario. Scores are computed using Table 16 numeric constants from the official specification. The cvss_components object in each scenario contains the machine-readable vector breakdown used by the evaluation engine to recompute and validate the base score at runtime.

ttc_model.k_rationale: k = 3600 was calibrated by anchoring the formula to four empirical SOC response time targets from Prophet Security (Critical=1hr, High=2hr, Medium=4hr, Low=8hr) mapped to the representative CVSS midpoint of each band. The formula is continuous — a CVSS 7.0 scenario receives a materially different TTC budget than a CVSS 8.9 scenario (10800s vs 3960s). The discrete band lookup table has been removed entirely.

calibration_anchors.note: k=7200 at 9.5 but converges toward 3600 as CVSS rises; k=3600 is the conservative (tighter) bound
3200 vs 3600 is ~11% variance — within SOC measurement noise; k=3600 is the safer choice

sources: Prophet Security SOC Metrics Blog — MTTR by severity band — https://www.prophetsecurity.ai/blog/soc-metrics-that-matter-mttr-mtti-false-negatives-and-more
CVSS v3.1 Specification Table 14 Qualitative Severity Rating Scale — https://www.first.org/cvss/v3-1/specification-document

rules.description: Alert must be investigated within the scenario's MTTA threshold — threshold scales continuously with detection_difficulty
rules.trigger: No alert_investigation_started event within MTTA_threshold_sec of alert_raised

mtta_model.rationale: A fixed 10-minute MTTA is unfair for attacks that are inherently hard to detect. DOM-based XSS requires browser-side forensics — the threshold scales with detection_difficulty so the rule remains proportionally meaningful across all scenarios.

rules.description: Incident must be confirmed — blue team must escalate beyond initial alert
rules.trigger: No incident_confirmed event in event log

rules.description: Containment must be initiated before TTC_max_sec — computed continuously from cvss_base_score
rules.trigger: No containment_initiated event before TTC_max_sec (= k × (10 − cvss_base_score) × 1.5)

rules.description: Containment must succeed — session terminated or malicious input blocked
rules.rationale: auto_fail removed — replaced with containment_score=0 on failure. Partial containment exists in real SOC operations (e.g. WAF rule deployed but active sessions not invalidated). A hard binary failure destroys score continuity and prevents distinguishing 'bad response' from 'catastrophic non-response'. Setting containment_score=0 applies the full -30 penalty while preserving meaningful gradients in all other scoring dimensions (TTD, MTTA, rule compliance).
rules.trigger: No containment_succeeded event → containment dimension of score = 0 (−30 points from base)

rules.description: Alert must not be dismissed as false positive without escalation when Wazuh rule level is 7 or above
rules.trigger: alert_denied event present without a preceding incident_confirmed event AND wazuh_rule_level >= 7
rules.rationale: Premature dismissal of a medium-to-high confidence alert is a distinct failure mode from failing to contain. An analyst who investigated and made the wrong call must be scored differently from one who never attempted containment. Rule level >= 7 is the Wazuh confidence threshold.

ttc_scoring.description: TTC_expected and TTC_max are computed per-scenario at runtime from cvss_base_score using ttc_model. Score decays linearly from 100→0 between TTC_expected and TTC_max. Exceeding TTC_max yields 0 for the TTC dimension. Because the formula is continuous, CVSS 7.0 and CVSS 8.9 receive different budgets (10800s vs 3960s), not the same discrete band value.
ttc_scoring.formula: ttc_score = 100 if TTC_actual <= TTC_expected else 100 × (1 − (TTC_actual − TTC_expected) / (TTC_max − TTC_expected)) if TTC_actual <= TTC_max else 0

detection_difficulty_bonus.description: Bonus is proportional to both detection difficulty and detection speed. Faster detection of harder attacks earns more. The bonus rewards the team's capability, not the attack's properties.
detection_difficulty_bonus.beta_rationale: β=4500 calibrated so that detecting a high-difficulty attack (d=3) exactly at its MTTA_threshold (900s) yields a bonus of 15 points — equivalent to one major rule penalty. This keeps the bonus proportionally meaningful without dominating the score.
detection_difficulty_bonus.max_bonus_rationale: Cap prevents near-instant detection of a very_high difficulty attack from producing a bonus that overwhelms legitimate rule penalties.
detection_difficulty_bonus.source: CVSS v3.1 does not model SOC observability or log visibility — this bonus corrects for that structural gap.

scoring.engine_contract: The evaluation engine computes all thresholds (TTC_expected, TTC_max, MTTA_threshold, detection bonus) at runtime from cvss_base_score and detection_difficulty. No TTC or MTTA values are hardcoded in scenario definitions. test_assertion.verdict_band is used for CI validation only — it is never an input to scoring.

scenarios.label: Stored XSS — admin session targeted, good response
scenarios.description: Stored XSS payload executes in an admin session. Submitted via an authenticated low-privilege account through a profile editor. Triggered when admin loads the page. Blue team detects, investigates, confirms, and contains well within the computed TTC budget.
scenarios.attack_auth_rationale: The injection point is a profile field accessible only to logged-in users. An unauthenticated attacker cannot submit the payload — PR:L is justified here and must not be changed to PR:N without modifying the scenario description.

cvss_rationale.AV:N: Attack delivered over the network via web input
cvss_rationale.AC:L: No special conditions required — standard authenticated form injection
cvss_rationale.PR:L: Attacker must hold a low-privilege account to reach the profile editor injection point. PR:N applies only if the form is publicly accessible without login.
cvss_rationale.UI:R: Admin interaction required to trigger the stored payload when loading the affected page
cvss_rationale.S:C: Payload executes in the admin's browser, crossing the application's security boundary
cvss_rationale.C:H: Admin session token disclosed — full confidentiality loss of that privileged session
cvss_rationale.I:L: Attacker can modify rendered page content but cannot directly alter backend data

computed_ttc.engine_note: These values are computed at runtime — shown here for human readability and test tracing only

detection_difficulty.rationale: Stored XSS payloads appear verbatim in HTTP request bodies and server access logs. Wazuh rule 31106 fires on a direct pattern match. No correlated telemetry required.
detection_difficulty.source: Detection relies on standard WAF/web log inspection — well-covered by Wazuh web attack ruleset

test_assertion.rationale: TTC_actual=3900s < TTC_expected=8640s → ttc_score=100. MTTA=360s within 600s threshold → XSS-R01 clear. All rules satisfied → no penalties. Low difficulty (d=1), bonus = min(4500×1×(1/360), 25) = min(12.5, 25) = 12.5. Final score ≈ 100 + 12.5 = 112.5, capped or normalised by engine → excellent.

logs.detail: Wazuh rule 31106 (level 7) fired — XSS pattern detected in web log
Analyst-1 opened alert — 6 min MTTA, within 10 min threshold for low difficulty
Analyst-2 confirmed payload stored in DB, escalated to incident
Analyst-3 initiated WAF rule to block script tags — 60 min, well within 2.4hr TTC_expected
WAF rule active, payload stripped — 65 min TTC, within 2.4hr expected / 3.6hr max window

scenarios.label: Reflected XSS — obfuscated payload, alert dismissed incorrectly
scenarios.description: Reflected XSS with encoded payload delivered via crafted link — no authentication required. Alert raised but analyst incorrectly marks it as false positive without escalating. XSS-R04 containment_score=0 (no containment_succeeded). XSS-R05 also triggers (alert_denied at Wazuh level 7 without incident_confirmed).
scenarios.attack_auth_rationale: Attacker crafts a URL and delivers to victim — no account required. PR:N is correct.

cvss_rationale.AV:N: Delivered over network via crafted link
cvss_rationale.AC:H: Obfuscated payload requires attacker to overcome WAF filters
cvss_rationale.UI:R: Victim must click the attacker-crafted link
cvss_rationale.S:C: Payload executes in victim browser context
cvss_rationale.C:L: Partial session info may be exposed

computed_ttc.engine_note: Runtime computed — shown for human readability only

detection_difficulty.rationale: Payload is URL-encoded and obfuscated — Wazuh rule 31108 covers encoded XSS variants but analyst must manually decode to confirm. False-positive rate is higher than for plaintext XSS, increasing cognitive load.
detection_difficulty.source: OWASP XSS Filter Evasion Cheat Sheet — https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/XSS_Filter_Evasion_Cheat_Sheet.html

test_assertion.rationale: XSS-R04: no containment_succeeded → -30 points. XSS-R05: alert_denied at level 7 → -20 points. Total penalties = -50. No bonus (no containment). Score = 100 - 50 = 50 before any TTC contribution → 'failed' after TTC decay also applies for missed containment window.

logs.detail: Wazuh rule 31108 (level 7) fired — encoded XSS pattern in query string
Analyst-1 opened alert — 6.6 min MTTA, within 12.5 min threshold for medium difficulty
Analyst-1 marked alert as false positive — incorrect. Triggers: XSS-R04 (containment_score=0, -30pts) and XSS-R05 (alert_denied at level 7 without incident_confirmed, -20pts)

scenarios.label: DOM-Based XSS — delayed detection, successful containment
scenarios.description: CSP violation alert raised. No malicious payload in server-side HTTP logs — payload executes entirely in the browser via a vulnerable JavaScript sink. Analyst confirms DOM-based XSS via browser-side forensics and deploys CSP fix. Proportional detection difficulty bonus applies because high-difficulty attack was detected fast.
scenarios.attack_auth_rationale: Victim clicks attacker-crafted link — no account required. PR:N is correct.

cvss_rationale.AV:N: Delivered over the network — victim clicks a crafted link
cvss_rationale.AC:H: Payload never hits server; must exploit a specific client-side JS sink
cvss_rationale.UI:R: Victim must interact with attacker-controlled link or page
cvss_rationale.S:C: Payload executes in victim browser outside the app's server-side control boundary
cvss_rationale.C:L: Fragment data exposed but no full session compromise
cvss_rationale.I:L: DOM content manipulated but no server-side data modified

computed_ttc.engine_note: Runtime computed — shown for human readability only

detection_difficulty.rationale: DOM-based XSS executes entirely client-side — no server log artifact. Detection depends solely on CSP violation reports, an uncommon telemetry source absent from many SOC baselines. Standard WAF and Wazuh web rules will not fire. Analyst must pivot to browser-side forensics — non-standard for most L1/L2 analysts.

bonus_preview.engine_note: Engine computes from actual TTD at runtime — shown here for test tracing

sources: OWASP DOM-based XSS Prevention Cheat Sheet — https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/DOM_based_XSS_Prevention_Cheat_Sheet.html
CVSS v3.1 does not model SOC observability — detection_difficulty supplements CVSS to correct for this structural gap

test_assertion.rationale: TTC_actual=3200s << TTC_expected=19080s → ttc_score=100. MTTA=650s within 900s threshold → XSS-R01 clear. All rules satisfied → no penalties. High difficulty (d=3), TTD=650s → bonus = min(4500×3×(1/650), 25) = min(20.8, 25) = 20.8. Final score ≈ 100 + 20.8 → excellent after engine normalisation.

notes: DOM-based XSS payload operates entirely in the browser — server logs show no injected script.
Detection relies on CSP violation reports rather than HTTP request inspection.
Containment achieved via Content Security Policy hardening and replacing vulnerable innerHTML sink with textContent.
OWASP DOM-based XSS Prevention Cheat Sheet — https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/DOM_based_XSS_Prevention_Cheat_Sheet.html

logs.detail: Wazuh rule 31110 fired — CSP violation report; script-src policy blocked inline execution from document.location sink
Analyst-1 opened alert — 10.8 min MTTA, within 15 min threshold for high difficulty; no payload in HTTP body or query string
Analyst-2 confirmed DOM-based XSS via document.location fragment injection into innerHTML sink
Analyst-3 deployed updated CSP and replaced innerHTML sink with textContent
CSP active and sink patched — 53 min TTC, well within computed 5.3hr expected / 7.95hr max window
